# v0.1.5 — Working-directory files panel

## Features

- **Resizable working-directory files panel in chat** — The chat view gains a collapsible side panel that lists the files in the session's working directory, so you can browse and open project files without leaving the conversation. The panel is resizable and remembers its width (and which folders you've expanded) across sessions, and it adapts to the available space: it renders inline when the panel is wide enough and folds into a floating popover when the chat content gets narrow — staying correct inside multi-panel layouts and tracking live panel/chat resizes. Backed by a new `scanWorkingDirectory` RPC, with all UI strings localized across the 7 supported languages. (`36bfe600`, `854d4148`, `b2e5115b`, `e0cf540d`, `ac526cdd`, `f47d371d`)

## Improvements

- None.

## Bug Fixes

- **Opening a large JSON file no longer freezes the app** — The JSON file preview (`@uiw/react-json-view`) and its code-viewer fallback (`ShikiCodeViewer`) are not virtualized, so opening a JSON file with tens of thousands of lines rendered tens of thousands of DOM nodes synchronously and hung the main thread. Added defense-in-depth size guards: above 256 KB the JSON tree skips the full-tree re-parse and collapses to its top level (drill in node-by-node); above 2 MB the file falls back to the read-only code viewer entirely; and the code viewer itself drops syntax highlighting and the per-line gutter above 256 KB, rendering a single plain `<pre>` text node. The last guard also fixes the same freeze for large code, text, and log files — not just JSON. (`0897b3d7`)

## Breaking Changes

- None.
